w:r.w:t: Describe it. Forge it. Ship it.
Full API Architecture · Database Schema · Route Handlers · AI Integration · DevOps Pipeline

Forge AI Builder is a Next.js 14 application where the backend lives entirely within Next.js Route Handlers (App Router). There is no separate Express or NestJS server. All backend logic — Salesforce OAuth, AI chat, deployment, Jira, Git, and billing — runs as serverless functions co-located with the frontend on Vercel.
Serverless-first — all API routes are Next.js Route Handlers deployed as Vercel Edge/Serverless Functions
Org-isolated — every Supabase query is scoped to the authenticated user_id via Row-Level Security (RLS)
Async deployment — Salesforce Metadata API calls are long-running (45–90s); handled with Supabase Realtime streaming
Atomic action counter — Redis INCR prevents race conditions on subscription plan limits
AI prompt caching — org metadata injected into every Claude call; prompt caching reduces API cost 35–45%
app/api/orgs/[id]/route.ts — Get / delete a specific org
app/api/orgs/[id]/health/route.ts — Run / fetch org health check
app/api/git/commit/route.ts — Commit SFDX metadata to branch
lib/salesforce.ts — JSforce client factory + token refresh
lib/ai.ts — Claude API helpers + prompt builder
lib/redis.ts — Upstash Redis client + action counter
User authentication uses Supabase Auth (email/password + magic link). The session JWT is stored in an HTTP-only cookie by Supabase SSR helpers.
Reads session cookie, validates JWT via Supabase Auth, redirects unauthenticated users to /login
Creates Supabase server client using cookies() — RLS automatically scopes all queries to auth.uid()
Exports createServerClient() wrapper with cookie integration for use in all Route Handlers
Salesforce uses OAuth 2.0 Web Server Flow. The Connected App is registered in the Forge ISV Partner Org.
Redirect user to Salesforce /authorize endpoint with client_id, redirect_uri, scope=api+refresh_token+full
Receive code param → POST to Salesforce /token → get access_token + refresh_token
AES-256-GCM encrypt both tokens → store in orgs table with instance_url, org_id, user_id
JSforce auto-refresh: on 401 response, exchange refresh_token for new access_token → re-encrypt and update DB
createSalesforceClient(orgId) — reads from DB, decrypts token, returns configured JSforce connection
TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY — 32-byte AES key (never in source code)
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY — For server-side RLS bypass (admin ops only)

Every Route Handler that touches Salesforce calls createSalesforceClient(orgId, userId). This function: fetches the encrypted org record from Supabase, decrypts both tokens, initialises a JSforce Connection, and registers an onRefresh callback to save the new token back to the database.
Triggered on first org connect and on manual refresh. This is the most complex backend operation.
Returns all metadata type names available in this org
For each type, list all members (API names). Uses Bulk API 2.0 for orgs with 1000+ objects
SELECT Id, Name, Body FROM ApexClass — reads full source code for governor limit scanning
Chunk each metadata item into text snippets → embed → store in Supabase pgvector with org_id namespace
Set metadata_synced_at, object_count, field_count, apex_class_count, flow_count
SELECT from orgs WHERE user_id = auth.uid(). Returns alias, type, sync status, health score.
Called by OAuth callback. Stores encrypted tokens. Triggers metadata ingestion as background job.
Returns org metadata counts, sync status, connected pipelines.
Deletes org record + all pgvector embeddings for that org_id namespace.
Re-runs ingestion pipeline. Returns job_id for polling.
Returns stored health JSON. If stale (>24h), triggers fresh analysis.
Runs 7 SOQL + Tooling API queries → Claude Haiku analysis → stores result → returns structured JSON.
This is the core AI endpoint. It streams Claude Sonnet responses token-by-token to the frontend using Server-Sent Events (SSE).
Verify session. Load subscription tier. Check action counter via Redis.
POST to Anthropic API with short prompt: classify intent as metadata-read / build / question / health. ~200ms.
Embed user message → cosine similarity search → retrieve top 10 most relevant org metadata chunks for this org.
Inject: org alias, org type, retrieved objects/fields/Apex/Flows, governor limit rules, Forge instructions.
anthropic.messages.stream({ model: claude-sonnet-4-5, system, messages }) → pipe to ReadableStream.
On stream end: extract <plan>[...] JSON from response text. Store in messages.implementation_plan.
INSERT conversation message (role=assistant, content, implementation_plan, confidence_score).
Atomic increment of action:{user_id}:{YYYY-MM} counter. Checked against tier limit on next request.
The frontend uses the native EventSource API (or a ReadableStream reader) to consume the stream:
Runs 7 independent queries against the org, feeds results to Claude Haiku, returns structured JSON matching the SDD §5.3 schema.
Tooling API: ApexClass source scan for SOQL/DML in loops
REST API: FlowDefinition WHERE Status != Active
REST API: Profile WHERE PermissionsModifyAllData = true
REST API: Flow best practice check (fault paths, null checks)
Metadata API: retrieve all Profiles for security scan

This is the most critical Route Handler. It is only reachable after the human approval step in the UI. The middleware verifies the approval record exists with a human timestamp before any JSforce call is made.
Check approved_by IS NOT NULL and approved_at is within 30 minutes. Block if missing.
INSERT with status=deploying, git_branch, jira_ticket_id. Returns deployment_id.
Execute step → write result to deployment_steps table → Supabase Realtime broadcasts to frontend.
Compile Apex → check CompilationResult → run tests → check coverage >= 75%. Auto-rollback if tests fail.
Package XML → deploy() → poll DeployResult every 3 seconds → write status updates to Realtime.
Claude verifies each acceptance criterion via follow-up Salesforce API queries. Returns 0-100 confidence score.
UPDATE deployments SET acceptance_criteria_results, confidence_score, status=success.
Retrieve pre-deployment state of changed items → store as JSONB in deployments.rollback_metadata. Available 24h.
The frontend subscribes to a Supabase Realtime channel on the deployment_steps table, filtered by deployment_id. Each step completion is a DB INSERT that Realtime broadcasts in real time.
updateDeploymentLog(payload.new); // renders step card in UI
Verify deployed_at + 24h > NOW(). Return 403 if expired.
Read deployments.rollback_metadata JSONB — the pre-deployment org state.
JSforce metadata.deploy() with the pre-deployment package XML.
INSERT into audit_log: action=rollback, metadata includes original + rollback deployment IDs.
Resend email to user: rollback complete. Jira comment added if ticket linked.
Initiate Atlassian OAuth 2.0 (3LO) flow — redirect to Atlassian /authorize
Receive code → exchange for tokens → encrypt → store in jira_connections table
Create Jira issue via REST API POST /rest/api/3/issue — called after plan approval
Receive Jira webhook (ticket approved) → validate JWT → trigger deployment pipeline
Read active sprint issues from Jira board — filtered by forge-ai label for board view screen
When a Jira ticket transitions to an approved state, Atlassian sends a signed webhook to Forge. This triggers the full build pipeline automatically.
Validate Atlassian webhook JWT signature using JIRA_CLIENT_SECRET. Return 401 if invalid.
Only proceed if webhook.issue.fields.status.name matches configured trigger (e.g. 'Approved for Dev').
Find jira_connections record for this Atlassian site URL. Check auto_deploy_trigger setting.
Parse description, acceptance criteria, target org, metadata change spec from Jira issue body.
POST to internal /api/chat with extracted spec + orgId. Get implementation plan back.
If auto_deploy_trigger = sandbox: call /api/deploy with auto-approval for sandbox org only.
POST comment to Jira ticket: 'Forge AI is building this. View progress: [forge.ai/deploy/...]'
Store GitHub access token in git_connections
Commit each SFDX metadata file to branch
Create PR with AI evaluation report as body
Get files changed in PR for diff view
Merge PR to main on Production approval
Org health, metadata explorer, AI chat (plans only)
Full AI implementation, deployment, rollback, history
All Starter + Jira, Git sync, multi-org pipeline, 5 seats
White-label, dedicated support, SLA, custom training
Create Stripe Checkout Session for plan upgrade. Redirect user to Stripe-hosted checkout page.
Create Stripe Customer Portal session. User manages plan, payment method, invoices.
Stripe webhook handler — handles subscription updates, payment failures, invoice payment success.
Update users.subscription_tier. Reset action_count. Send welcome email via Resend.
Sync new plan tier to users table. Update feature flags in Redis.
Start 7-day grace period. Send payment failure email. Do not downgrade immediately.
Confirm subscription active. Reset monthly action counter in Redis for new billing period.
Downgrade user to Free tier. Revoke deploy access. Keep org connections in read-only mode.
Every API request that consumes an AI action passes through this middleware chain in order:
SELECT subscription_tier FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid()
COUNT orgs WHERE user_id → compare to tier limit
Redis GET action:{userId}:{month} → compare to tier limit
403 Forbidden: feature name + upgrade tier required
Redis sliding window: 10 req/minute per user
429 Too Many Requests: retry-after header set
One row per signed-up user. Managed by Supabase Auth + custom columns.
user_id, org_alias, instance_url, access_token (encrypted), refresh_token (encrypted), org_type
Connected Salesforce orgs. Multiple per user.
AI chat sessions. One conversation per feature request or Jira ticket.
Individual chat messages. JSONB plan column stores structured deploy steps.
conversation_id, org_id, status, git_branch, rollback_metadata (JSONB), acceptance_criteria_results (JSONB)
One row per deployment attempt. Includes rollback snapshot.
deployment_id, step_number, status, api_used, duration_ms, error_message
Supabase Realtime source table. One row per deploy step — streamed live.
Connected Jira workspaces. One per user per Atlassian site.
Connected GitHub/GitLab repos. One per user per repo.
Immutable record of all actions. Never deleted. Used for compliance.
All tables have RLS enabled. No cross-tenant data leakage is possible at the database level, regardless of application code bugs.
USING (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM conversations c WHERE c.id = conversation_id AND c.user_id = auth.uid()))
USING (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM deployments d WHERE d.id = deployment_id AND d.user_id = auth.uid()))
Each connected org gets its own namespace in pgvector. Embeddings are generated by OpenAI text-embedding-3-small and stored in a metadata_embeddings table.
Namespace isolation — always filter by org_id
Full metadata JSON for injection into Claude prompt
Supabase project setup — create all tables with RLS
Next.js project init with App Router + TypeScript
pgvector table + cosine similarity search helper
System prompt builder with org context injection
Plan JSON extraction from Claude response
Org health check — 7 queries + Claude Haiku analysis
Tooling API Apex deploy + test runner
Audit log writes on every action
Jira webhook receiver with JWT validation
Auto-create Pull Request with AI eval report
PR diff view — fetch changed files
All Salesforce, Jira, GitHub tokens encrypted before DB write
RLS on all Supabase tables — user_id = auth.uid()
Hard-coded in middleware — cannot be bypassed via API
Approval must be < 30 minutes old before deploy fires
Validate Atlassian JWT on every webhook — 401 if invalid
INCR is atomic — prevents race condition on plan limits
API keys in env vars only
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY never in source or DB
Coverage < 75% → automatic rollback before user sees success
Next.js default CORS policy. No public API access.
Initiate Salesforce OAuth — redirect to SF authorize
OAuth callback — exchange code for tokens
Save org after OAuth (called by callback)
Fetch / run fresh org health check
AI chat streaming (SSE) — core AI endpoint
Poll deployment status (fallback if Realtime fails)
Read active sprint from Jira board
Forge AI Builder · Backend Implementation Plan v1.0 · CONFIDENTIAL
